BW: Kalahari sand and fossil pans; teak savanna between seasonal salt-crusted flats
BY: Smoothed plains and peat fens; cool-summer lake districts between broad forest belts
BZ: Granitic inland highlands; eastward folds end at a long offshore barrier reef shelf
CA: Shield bedrock with boreal lakes; rain-shadow basins west of wide interior plains
CD: Rift escarpment above tangled rift lakes; montane forest on wet western scarps
CF: Tropical woodland basin; inselbergs on humid seasonal plains between low granite hills
CG: Atlantic coastal strip backed by dense lowland rainforest on the humid equator
CH: Alpine chains, deep glacial troughs; lake basins between sharp peaks and avalanche paths
CI: Coastal lagoon strings; inland wet forest on weathered basement plateaus
CL: Icefields on the cordillera; fjords where westerly storms meet glacier toes offshore
CM: Active volcanic line above rainforest; savanna southward on high rift-flank scarps
CN: Eastern low alluvium; loess plateau west; high tableland beyond sharp rain-shadow fronts
CO: Triple cordilleras with deep basins; river trench draining toward the Caribbean rim
CR: Volcanic spine; wet Pacific cloud forest on windward slopes, drier Caribbean lee basins
CU: Long limestone island arc; north shore shelves onto a wide carbonate reef platform
CV: Arid volcanic cones on trade-wind isles; rocky coasts with few perennial streams
CY: Twin limestone massifs frame central basins; pocket bays on a dry Mediterranean-type rim
CZ: Ring uplands around interior hill basins; northern lowlands thread sandstone escarpments
DE: Northern plain; forested central uplands; Alpine foreland south of calcareous ranges
DJ: Ocean-linked rift; spreading crust meets gulf head with salt flats and cinder cones
DK: Moraine islands; tidal Wadden shallows open to brackish inner seas on smoothed plains
DM: Volcanic peaks core the island; dense windward rainforest on east Atlantic slopes
DO: Cordilleran spine splits rainfall; karst uplands with limestone cave networks inland
DZ: Tell folds meet a Mediterranean littoral; snow-capped high range towers over dry piedmont
